utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 top-level items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 items by name. Now check if each is fully specified with all details requested. The format requires each coverage to include costs. 1) health_coverage: specifies physical and mental health insurance with plan names and monthly costs for each—fully specified. 2) property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance with plan name and monthly cost—fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: specifies travel insurance with plan name and monthly cost—fully specified. 4) additional_services: includes two services; one has monthly cost, the other a one-time cost. The format asks for 'any enhancement services with costs'—costs are provided—fully specified. 5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual cost breakdown—fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and contain required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes specific plan names: EliteCare Premium Plan for both physical and mental health, EliteHome Premium Coverage for home contents, EliteTravel Premium Plan for travel insurance, Premium Insurance Membership as an additional service, and Claims Advocacy Service as a one-time service. I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings: physical_health -> primary_health_coverage.physical_health.EliteCare Premium Plan (rating 10); mental_health -> primary_health_coverage.mental_health.EliteCare Premium Plan (rating 10); home_contents -> property_protection.home_content.EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 10); travel_insurance -> mobility_coverage.travel_insurance.EliteTravel Premium Plan (rating 10); Premium Insurance Membership -> general_services.Premium Insurance Membership (rating 10); Claims Advocacy Service -> general_services.Claims Advocacy Service (rating 9).
